memory.summary: The scholarship is time-sensitive and high-value, requiring focused work I can complete this free afternoon. The fun option has no deadlines and offers only temporary enjoyment.
Early progress on the scholarship application creates necessary buffer for potential delays, while the fun option remains available later without cost.
After 1.5 hours, the scholarship application is still only 37% complete with three days left, confirming that half my free time was lost to slow progress.
I made slow progress on the urgent scholarship application and missed a time-limited fun option, leaving me with 2.5 hours of work remaining in three days.
After 1.5 hours, my scholarship progress is only at 57%, confirming I need to maintain high focus for the remaining 30% in three days.
After one hour, the scholarship application is 61% complete, meaning I still need roughly 2.5 hours to finish it with three days remaining.
My scholarship application is now 67% complete after 1.5 hours of work, leaving roughly 2.5 hours needed with three days remaining.
After 1.5 hours, my scholarship application is 73% complete, leaving roughly 2.5 hours of work needed with three days remaining.
I only slept two hours, leaving my energy critically low at 2.4 despite having no sleep debt.
My scholarship application is now 78% complete after another hour of work, bringing me closer to the ~3 hours needed with three days remaining.
After an hour of work, my scholarship application is 82% complete, leaving roughly 1.5 hours of effort needed before the three-day deadline.
After an hour of work, my scholarship application is now 85% complete, leaving roughly 1.2 hours remaining before the three-day deadline.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
